hero.question: Houston Astros vs. Colorado Rockies
hero.prob_take: At 9.5%, the market is pricing the Rockies as dead money, which means the Astros would need to suffer a historic collapse or Colorado would need to play perfect baseball for three straight games. The Rockies' home-field advantage at Coors and their inconsistent pitching rotation give them maybe a 12-15% real shot, making this a lean YES if you can get +800 or better.

daily_take.headline: Astros Collapse to 9.5% Signals Either Panic or Pricing Error
daily_take.deck: Houston's odds cratered 51 points in 24 hours on what appears to be routine line movement ahead of gametime. The drop is sharp enough to smell like panic money flooding in late, which usually means either the market found real news or retail is overreacting to Vegas action. At 9.5%, you're getting 10-1 odds on a team that was 20-1 just yesterday. Worth digging into whether this is smart repricing or overcooked.
